1: # Portfolio Website — Agent Handoff
2: 
3: > **Project:** Joel Alfred Israel's ThinkPad Terminal Portfolio  
4: > **Location:** `d:\portfolio-draft`  
5: > **Stack:** Astro 5.x + React 19 + xterm.js 5.5  
6: > **Status:** Functional, needs polish + deployment  
7: > **Last Updated:** 2026-06-14T19:28 IST
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## Quick Start
12: 
13: ```bash
14: cd d:\portfolio-draft
15: npm run dev          # → http://localhost:4321
16: npx astro check      # → 1 cosmetic TS warning (safe to ignore)
17: npx astro build      # → Production build to dist/
18: ```
19: 
20: ---
21: 
22: ## Architecture
23: 
24: ```
25: User opens page
26:   → LaptopShell.astro (3D closed ThinkPad lid, CSS perspective)
27:   → Click/keypress triggers lid open animation (CSS rotateX)
28:   → BootOverlay.tsx (POST boot text sequence)
29:   → Terminal.tsx (xterm.js, client:only="react")
30:      → commands.ts (command dispatcher, 20+ commands)
31:      → easter.ts (hidden easter egg commands)
32:      → neofetch.ts (system info display)
33:      → chatbot.ts (AI chat via Ollama)
34:      → snake.ts (playable Snake game)
35:      → filesystem.ts (virtual file system)
36:      → themes.ts (4 color themes)
37: ```
38: 
39: ### Key Design Decisions
40: - **Astro Islands**: Only `Terminal.tsx` and `BootOverlay.tsx` are hydrated React. Everything else is static Astro/CSS.
41: - **`client:only="react"`**: xterm.js cannot SSR — must use `client:only`, NOT `client:load`.
42: - **No Tailwind**: All CSS is vanilla, organized by component.
43: - **ThinkPad Aesthetic**: Brushed metal gradients, pu
<truncated 4086 bytes>
tOS project |
126: | No favicon or OG images | Low | Generate and add to `public/` |
127: 
128: ---
129:

173: **Joel Alfred Israel** — 2nd-year CS @ VIT Chennai, CGPA 8.09/10  
174: - Published researcher (WASM, ICTMIM 2026)

The page consists of: 24: 25: 1. **Static shell** — 3D laptop frame + CRT effects (zero JavaScript, pure HTML/CSS) 26: 2. **Interactive islands** — Three React components hydrated client-side 27: 3. **Terminal engine** — Pure TypeScript modules with no React dependencies 28: 29: The key architectural decision is **separating presentation (React) from logic (TypeScript modules)**. The terminal engine (`src/terminal/`) is framework-agnostic — it could be used with any terminal emulator or testing harness. 30: 31: --- 32: 33: ## Component Diagram 34: 35: ```mermaid 36: graph TB 37: subgraph "Astro Static Shell" 38: Layout["Layout.astro

(Page orchestrator)"] 40: Laptop["LaptopShell.astro
(3D CSS frame)"] 41: CRT["CRTScreen.astro
../styles/global.css'; /* Must be first */ 342: @import '../styles/laptop.css'; 343: @import '../styles/crt.css'; 344: @import '../styles/terminal.css'; 345: @import '../styles/boot.css'; /* Must be last (highest specificity) */ 346: ``` 347: 348: Astro bundles these at build time — no runtime cost. 349: 350: ### CSS Variables 351: 352: Global design tokens are defined in `global.css` and consumed throughout: 353: 354: ```css 355: :root { 356: --amber: #ff8c00; 357: --amber-dim: rgba(255, 140, 0, 0.3); 358: --bg-dark: #0a0a0f; 359: --font-mono: 'Share Tech Mono', 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; 360: } 361: ``` 362: 363: --- 364: 365: ## Build & Bundle Strategy 366: 367: ### Astro Config 368: 369: ```javascript 370: // astro.config.mjs 371: export default defineConfig({ 372: integrations: [react()], 373: output: 'static', // Pre-rendered HTML, no server 374: vite: { 375: optimizeDeps: { 376: include: ['@xterm/xterm', '@xterm/addon-fit', '@xterm/addon-web-links'], 377: }, 378: }, 379: }); 380: ``` 381: 382: ### TypeScript Path Aliases 383: 384: ```json 385: // tsconfig.json 386: { 387: "paths": { 388: "@components/*": ["src/components/*"], 389: "@terminal/*": ["src/terminal/*"], 390: "@styles/*": ["src/styles/*"], 391: "@data/*": ["src/data/*"] 392: } 393: } 394: ``` 395: 396: ### Output 397: 398: ``` 399: dist/ 400: ├── index.html # Pre-rendered page 401: ├── _astro/ # Hashed JS/CSS bundles 402: │ ├── Terminal.xxxxx.js # Terminal island bundle 403: │ ├── BootOverlay.xxxxx.js 404: │ ├── AudioBar.xxxxx.js 405: │ └── index.xxxxx.css # All CSS merged 406: ├── data/ 407: │ ├── portfolio.json # Copied from public/ 408: │ └── ezra_system.md 409: ├── sounds/ 410: │ └── ambient.mp3 411: └── favicon.svg 412: ``` 413: The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

1: # Component Documentation
2: 
3: > Component reference for **Joel's CLI Portfolio**
4: > Built with Astro + React + xterm.js
5: 
6: ---
7: 
8: ## Architecture Overview
9: 
10: ```
11: src/
12: ├── layouts/
13: │   └── Layout.astro              # Base HTML wrapper
14: ├── components/
15: │   ├── LaptopShell.astro          # 3D laptop frame (static)
16: │   ├── CRTScreen.astro            # CRT effects wrapper (static)
17: │   ├── BootOverlay.tsx            # Boot sequence (React island)
18: │   ├── Terminal.tsx               # xterm.js terminal (React island)
19: │   └── AudioBar.tsx               # Audio controls (React island)
20: ├── pages/
21: │   └── index.astro                # Main page composition
22: ├── styles/
23: │   ├── global.css                 # Reset + base typography
24: │   ├── laptop.css                 # 3D laptop + lid animation
25: │   ├── crt.css                    # Scanlines, vignette, LED
26: │   ├── terminal.css               # xterm.js mount overrides
27: │   └── boot.css                   # Boot overlay appearance
28: └── terminal/
29:     ├── config.ts                  # Centralized configuration
30:     ├── types.ts                   # Shared TypeScript interfaces
31:     ├── themes.ts                  # xterm.js color themes
32:     ├── commands.ts                # Command dispatcher
33:     ├── filesystem.ts              # Virtual filesystem (ls/cd/cat)
34:     ├── data.ts                    # Portfo
<truncated 9573 bytes>
reset, base typography, `::selection`, `skip-link`, reduced-motion |
314: | `laptop.css` | 3D perspective, lid-open animation (`@keyframes lidOpen`), keyboard/trackpad |
315: | `crt.css` | Scanlines, vignette, power LED pulse, phosphor glow, barrel filter ref |

320: 
321: ## Boot Sequence Diagram
322: 
323: ```mermaid
324: sequenceDiagram
325:     participant Page as index.astro
326:     participant Boot as BootOverlay
327:     participant Script as inline script
328:     participant Lid as #lid
329:     participant Term as Terminal
330: 
331:     Page->>Boot: client:load mount
332:     Boot->>Boot: setActive(true), fade in
333:     loop Each boot line
334:         Boot->>Boot: typewrite chars (rAF)
335:         Boot->>Boot: sleep(bootLineDelay)
336:     end
337:     Boot->>Boot: setActive(false), fade out
338:     Boot->>Script: window.__bootComplete()
339:     Script->>Lid: classList.add('open')
340:     Lid->>Lid: CSS @keyframes lidOpen
341:     Lid->>Script: animationend
342:     Script->>Term: classList.add('visible')
343:     Term->>Term: xterm focused, interactive
344: ```
345: 
346: ---

1: # 🤝 Contributing Guide
2: 
3: > How to contribute to Joel's CLI Portfolio.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## Table of Contents
8: 
9: - [Getting Started](#getting-started)
10: - [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
11: - [Coding Standards](#coding-standards)
12: - [File Naming Conventions](#file-naming-conventions)
13: - [Architecture Rules](#architecture-rules)
14: - [Adding Features](#adding-features)
15: - [Pull Request Process](#pull-request-process)
16: - [Documentation Requirements](#documentation-requirements)
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## Getting Started
21: 
22: ### Prerequisites
23: 
24: - **Node.js** ≥ 18.x
25: - **npm** ≥ 9.x
26: - A modern browser (Chrome/Firefox/Edge)
27: 
28: ### Setup
29: 
30: ```bash
31: # Clone and install
32: git clone https://github.com/Joel01010/portfolio.git
33: cd portfolio
34: npm install
35: 
36: # Start dev server
37: npm run dev
38: # → http://localhost:4321
39: 
40: # Run type checking
41: npm run check
42: ```
43: 
44: ### Key Directories
45: 
46: | Directory | What's there | Edit frequency |
47: |-----------|-------------|----------------|
48: | `src/terminal/` | Terminal engine (commands, filesystem, chatbot) | Most changes |
49: | `src/components/` | React islands + Astro components | Moderate changes |
50: | `src/styles/` | CSS files | Occasional |
51: | `src/data/` | Portfolio data, AI prompt | Content updates |
52: | `public/` | Static assets, runtime data | Rare |
53: | `legacy/` | Old codebase — **DO NOT EDIT** | Never |
54: 
55: ---
56: 
57: ## Development Workflow
58: 
59: ### Branch Strategy
60: 
61: ```
62: main          ← produ
<truncated 7918 bytes>
ts
285: 
286: If visual changes, include before/after screenshots.
287: ```
288: 
289: ### Review Criteria
290: 
291: - Does the code follow the [coding standards](#coding-standards)?
292: - Does it respect the [architecture rules](#architecture-rules)?
293: - Is it documented (JSDoc + comments for non-obvious logic)?
294: - Does it introduce any tech debt? If so, document it in [TECH_DEBT.md](TECH_DEBT.md).
295: 
296: ---
297: 
298: ## Documentation Requirements
299: 
300: ### Code Documentation
301: 
302: - **All exported functions** must have JSDoc comments
303: - **All exported interfaces/types** must have JSDoc on the declaration and each property
304: - **Complex logic** must have inline comments explaining *why*, not *what*
305: - **Module headers** should use `@fileoverview` JSDoc tag
306: 
307: ```typescript
308: /**
309:  * @fileoverview Virtual filesystem for the CLI portfolio.
310:  * @module filesystem
311:  */
312: 
313: /**
314:  * Resolve a relative path to an absolute path within the virtual FS.
315:  *
316:  * @param target - User-provided path (e.g. "..", "~", "projects")
317:  * @returns Resolved absolute path starting with "~"
318:  */
319: export function resolvePath(target: string): string {
320:   // Handle parent directory navigation
321:   if (target === '..') {
322:     return cwd.includes('/') ? cwd.split('/').slice(0, -1).join('/') : '~';
323:   }
324:   // ...
325: }
326: ```
327: 
328: ### Project Documentation
329: 
330: If you change behavior that's documented in these files, update them:
331: 
332: | Change Type | Update |
333: |-------------|--------|
334: | New command | [README.md](../README.md), `cmdHelp()` in commands.ts |
335: | New component | [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md), [README.md](../README.md) |
336: | New dependency | [README.md](../README.md) tech stack table |
337: | New tech debt | [TECH_DEBT.md](TECH_DEBT.md) |
338: | Breaking change | [MAINTENANCE.md](MAINTENANCE.md) |
339:

1: # 🔧 Maintenance Playbook
2: 
3: > Step-by-step recipes for common maintenance tasks.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## Table of Contents
8: 
9: - [Update Portfolio Data](#update-portfolio-data)
10: - [Add a New Project](#add-a-new-project)
11: - [Add a New Command](#add-a-new-command)
12: - [Add a New Theme](#add-a-new-theme)

14: - [Update Ezra's Personality](#update-ezras-personality)
15: - [Change Boot Sequence](#change-boot-sequence)
16: - [Update Neofetch Display](#update-neofetch-display)

23: ---
24: 
25: ## Update Portfolio Data
26: 
27: **File:** [`src/data/portfolio.json`](../src/data/portfolio.json)
28: 
29: Edit the JSON file. The structure is:
30: 
31: ```jsonc
32: {
33:   "owner": { "name": "", "handle": "", "title": "", ... },
34:   "about": ["line 1", "line 2", ...],
35:   "skills": { "Category": ["skill1", "skill2"] },
36:   "projects": [ { "slug": "", "name": "", "desc": "", "tech": [], "url": "", "year": 2025 } ],
37:   "contact": { "email": "", "github": "", "linkedin": "", "twitter": "" }
38: }
39: ```
40: 
41: **After editing:**
42: 1. Verify JSON is valid (no trailing commas, all strings quoted)
43: 2. Run `npm run dev` and test:
44:    - `about` command
45:    - `skills` command
46:    - `projects` command
47:    - `contact` command
48:    - `ls` → `cd projects` → `ls` → `cat <slug>.md
<truncated 11237 bytes>
ands work (run through the test checklist)
442: - [ ] CRT effects render properly
443: - [ ] Audio plays on user interaction
444: - [ ] Mobile layout is acceptable
445: - [ ] Open Graph image shows when sharing URL
446: 
447: ---
448: 
449: ## Troubleshooting
450: 
451: ### Boot sequence doesn't start
452: 
453: - Check browser console for JS errors
454: - Verify `BootOverlay.tsx` has `client:load` directive
455: - Check that CSS files are loading (inspect Network tab)
456: 
457: ### Terminal not appearing after boot
458: 
459: - Verify `window.__bootComplete` is defined (check inline script in `index.astro`)
460: - Check that `#lid` element exists and receives `.open` class
461: - Inspect `animationend` event firing on `#lid`
462: 
463: ### Commands show "Data not loaded yet"
464: 
465: - Check Network tab for `portfolio.json` fetch (should be 200)
466: - Verify `public/data/portfolio.json` exists and is valid JSON
467: - Check `data.ts` for fetch errors in console
468: 
469: ### Ezra returns errors
470: 
471: - Check `config.ts` for correct `mimoEndpoint` and `mimoApiKey`
472: - Check Network tab for API response status
473: - Try with a different API key or model
474: - Check CORS headers if using a custom endpoint
475: 
476: ### xterm.js not fitting container
477: 
478: - The FitAddon needs the container to have explicit dimensions
479: - Check that `#terminal-mount` has CSS `width` and `height` set
480: - Verify `requestAnimationFrame(() => fitAddon.fit())` runs after mount
481: 
482: ### CRT effects not showing
483: 
484: - Check that `crt.css` is loaded
485: - SVG filter requires the `#crt-barrel` filter to be in the DOM
486: - Check `prefers-reduced-motion` — effects may be disabled
487: 
488: ### Build fails with TypeScript errors
489: 
490: ```bash
491: # Run the checker for detailed errors
492: npm run check
493: 
494: # Common fix: unused imports
495: # Remove any import that's flagged by noUnusedLocals
496: ```
497:

1: # 🚧 Technical Debt Tracker
2: 
3: > Known issues, planned improvements, and optimization opportunities.  
4: > Last reviewed: 2026-06-14
5: 
6: ---
7: 
8: ## Priority Legend
9: 
10: | Priority | Meaning | Action Required |
11: |----------|---------|-----------------|
12: | **P0** | 🔴 Critical | Security risk or major UX issue — fix before production |
13: | **P1** | 🟡 Important | Significant improvement — should be addressed this quarter |
14: | **P2** | 🟢 Nice-to-have | Improvement or cleanup — address when convenient |
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## P0 — Critical
19: 
20: ### 🔴 API Key Exposed Client-Side
21: 
22: | | |
23: |---|---|
24: | **File** | [`src/terminal/config.ts`](../src/terminal/config.ts) |
25: | **Issue** | `mimoApiKey` is hardcoded in client-side config. Anyone can extract it from the JS bundle. |
26: | **Impact** | API key abuse, rate limiting, cost overrun |
27: | **Solution** | Route AI requests through a serverless proxy (Vercel/Netlify Edge Function) |
28: 
29: **Proposed fix:**
30: 
31: ```
32: Browser → POST /api/chat → Serverless Function → MiMo API
33:                               (key stored as env var)
34: ```
35: 
36: Create `src/pages/api/chat.ts` (Astro SSR endpoint) or use a Vercel/Netlify Edge Function:
37: 
38: ```typescript
39: // Example: src/pages/api/chat.ts (requires output: 'server' or 'hybrid')
40: export async function POST({ request }) {
41:   const body = await request.json();
42:   const res = await fetch(import.meta.env.MIMO_ENDPOINT, {
43:     method: 'POST',
44:     headers: {
45:       'Content-Type': 'application/jso
<truncated 8614 bytes>
 **Impact** | May cause lag on low-end devices or integrated GPUs |
288: | **Solution** | Detect performance and disable effects, or provide a toggle |
289: 
290: ---
291: 
292: ### 🟢 No `robots.txt` or `sitemap.xml`
293: 
294: | | |
295: |---|---|
296: | **Files** | Missing from `public/` |
297: | **Issue** | Search engines may not index the site optimally |
298: | **Solution** | Add `public/robots.txt` and use Astro's sitemap integration |
299: 
300: ---
301:

37: ### Path Aliases (use these in imports)

43: @data/*        → src/data/*
44: ```

53: The `legacy/` directory is a frozen archive of the
<truncated 10565 bytes>
|
314: | Install packages without checking bundle size | xterm.js is already heavy; be mindful |
315: | Add images to `src/` | Put static assets in `public/` |
316: | Use `innerHTML` | Use `textContent` or `document.createElement()` |
317: | Access `window.__bootComplete` in components | That's glue code in `index.astro` only |
318: | Change `output: 'static'` to `'server'` | Unless implementing the AI proxy (see TECH_DEBT.md) |

330: 2. Boot sequence plays

332: 4. Type `neofetch` — renders correctly
333: 
334: ### Full Regression Test (5 minutes)
335:

338: 3. `about` — shows bio
339: 4. `skills` — shows skills by category
340: 5. `projects` — shows all projects
341: 6. `contact` — shows contact info

343: 8. `cd ..` → `cd ~` — navigation works
344: 9. `neofetch` — ASCII art + info displays correctly
345: 10. `theme` — lists themes with active marker
346: 11. `theme green` → `theme amber` — theme switching works
347: 12. `clear` — clears screen
348: 13. `invalidcommand` — shows "command not found"
349: 14. Tab completion: type `he` + Tab → completes to `help `
350: 15. History: up arrow → recalls last command
351: 16. Ctrl+C → cancels input
352: 17. `npm run build` — builds without errors
353: 
354: ### Build Verification

357: npm run check   # TypeScript errors
358: npm run build   # Full static build
359: npm run preview # Serve and test the built site

2: 
3: > **Purpose:** Quick reference for Hermes Agent configuration, used by AgentOS setup scripts.
4: > **Source:** Research conducted 2026-06-14, Hermes v0.16.0
5: > **Last Updated:** 2026-06-14
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Installation
10: 
11: ```bash
12: # Linux / WSL2 / macOS
13: curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash
14: source ~/.bashrc
15: 
16: # Windows (PowerShell)
17: iex (irm https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.ps1)
18: ```
19: 
20: Installs: Python 3.11, Node.js, uv, ripgrep, ffmpeg, virtual environment.
21: 
22: ---
23: 
24: ## File Layout
25: 
26: ```
27: ~/.hermes/
28: ├── config.yaml       # Primary config
29: ├── .env              # API keys and secrets
30: ├── auth.json         # OAuth credentials
31: ├── skills/           # Custom + community skills
32: ├── memory/           # Persistent memory
33: │   ├── MEMORY.md     # Agent's notes (2,200 char limit)
34: │   └── USER.md       # User profile (1,375 char limit)
35: └── sessions/         # Past conversation logs (SQLite + FTS5)
36: ```
37: 
38: ---
39: 
40: ## CLI Commands (Complete)
41: 
42: | Command | Description |
43: |---|---|
44: | `hermes` / `hermes chat` | Start interactive chat |
45: | `hermes chat -q "prompt"` | Non-interactive single query |
46: | `hermes setup` | Setup wizard |
47: | `hermes setup --portal` | Nous Portal OAuth setup |
48: | `hermes model` | Switch model provider |
49: | `hermes gateway` | Start messaging gateway |
50: | `hermes gateway setup` | Configure gat
<truncated 1879 bytes>
115: └── templates/        # Optional: Output templates
116: ```
117: 
118: ### SKILL.md Format
119: ```yaml
120: ---

123: version: 1.0.0

165: ```ini
166: # /etc/wsl.conf
167: [automount]
168: options = "metadata,umask=22,fmask=11"
169: ```
170: 
171: ### Enable systemd
172: ```ini
173: # /etc/wsl.conf
174: [boot]
175: systemd=true
176: ```
177: 
178: Then: `wsl --shutdown` from PowerShell and restart.

185: - **Memory:** MEMORY.md (2,200 chars) + USER.md (1,375 chars) injected into system prompt

1: # AgentOS — Router Architecture Reference
2: 
3: > **Purpose:** Technical reference for the model routing system. For future agents maintaining AgentOS.
4: > **Last Updated:** 2026-06-14
5: 
6: ---
7: 
8: ## Design Decision: 3-Layer Routing
9: 
10: We chose a **3-layer architecture** based on 2026 production consensus:
11: 
12: ### Why Not Just One Layer?
13: 
14: | Approach | Latency | Accuracy | Cost |
15: |---|---|---|---|
16: | Rules only | 0ms | ~60% (brittle) | Free |
17: | LLM classifier only | 100-300ms | ~95% | API calls |
18: | Semantic embeddings only | 5-20ms | ~85% | Free (local) |
19: | **3-Layer hybrid** | **0-20ms avg** | **~97%** | **Mostly free** |
20: 
21: The hybrid approach handles **~95% of traffic** without any LLM call (rules + embeddings), and only uses the expensive LLM classifier for the ~5% ambiguous cases.
22: 
23: ---
24: 
25: ## Open Source Tools Evaluated
26: 
27: ### Selected
28: 
29: | Tool | Role | Why Chosen |
30: |---|---|---|
31: | **LiteLLM** (`BerriAI/litellm`) | Unified API proxy | OpenAI-compatible, handles failover/caching, YAML config, widely adopted |
32: | **Semantic Router** (`aurelio-labs/semantic-router`) | Layer 2 classifier | Sub-20ms, local embeddings, no API calls, great Python API |
33: | **Pydantic** | Data models | Type safety, JSON serialization, config validation |
34: 
35: ### Considered but Not Selected
36: 
37: | Tool | Why Not |
38: |---|---|
39: | **RouteLLM** (`lm-sys/RouteLLM`) | Powerful ML routers (MF, BERT) but overkill for our binary strong/weak needs. Keep as optional. |
40: | **TensorZero** | Rust-based (<
<truncated 716 bytes>
 **custom routing** via our 3-layer Python router, with LiteLLM as the proxy layer that handles failover + caching downstream.
59: 
60: ---
61: 
62: ## Cost Optimization: Key Insights
63: 
64: 1. **RouteLLM reports 40-85% cost savings** with strong/weak routing
65: 2. **Semantic caching** (LiteLLM built-in) eliminates repeated queries
66: 3. **SambaNova is free** for Joel — so "cost" mainly means latency and privacy
67: 4. **Confidence-based cascading**: Try cheap model first, escalate only if confidence is low
68: 
69: ---
70: 
71: ## Observability
72: 
73: ### Metrics to Track (via SQLite log)
74: 
75: | Metric | SQL Column | Why |
76: |---|---|---|
77: | Route distribution | `classified_by` | Detect drift from expected ratios |
78: | Latency per layer | `latency_ms` | Identify slow paths |
79: | Cache hit rate | `cache_hit` | Optimize cache TTL |
80: | Privacy override rate | `contains_sensitive_data` | Verify detection works |
81: | Provider fallback rate | (LiteLLM logs) | Identify availability issues |
82: 
83: ### Future: Langfuse Integration
84: 
85: When ready for production observability, add:
86: ```bash
87: pip install langfuse
88: ```
89: And configure in LiteLLM:
90: ```yaml
91: litellm_settings:
92:   callbacks: ["langfuse"]
93:   langfuse_host: "http://localhost:3000"  # Self-hosted Langfuse
94: ```

### 2. ARCHITECTURE.md (413 lines, 13 KB)
Detailed system architecture with Mermaid diagrams:
- **Three layers**: Static shell (zero JS), React islands (client-side), Terminal engine (pure TS)
- **Key decision**: Separation of presentation (React) from logic (TypeScript modules) — terminal engine is framework-agnostic
- **Rendering pipeline**: Strict boot sequence with ~5.5s total boot time (pre-boot 400ms → boot lines ~2.5s → post-boot 600ms → fade-out 550ms → lid open 1.2s → terminal fade-in 300ms)
- **Island hydration**: BootOverlay + Terminal = `client:load`; AudioBar = `client:idle`; LaptopShell/CRT/Layout = static (zero JS)
- **Data flow**: portfolio.json → data.ts (cached loader) → commands.ts/filesystem.ts/chatbot.ts. AI chatbot uses Xiaomi MiMo API (OpenAI-compatible format)
- **Terminal engine layers**: Terminal.tsx → commands.ts (interpreter) → feature modules (
<truncated 16014 bytes>
